questions.question_text: What must every real estate advertisement in New York include?
questions.correct_answer: The name of the licensed broker
distractors: The salesperson's personal cell phone number
The MLS listing number and property address
The property's most recent assessed tax value
questions.explanation: New York law prohibits blind ads. Every real estate advertisement must identify the licensed broker responsible for the ad, regardless of the medium used.

questions.question_text: A salesperson posts a listing on social media using only her team name and personal contact info. Is this compliant with NY advertising rules?
questions.correct_answer: No, the broker's name must also appear
distractors: Yes, team names satisfy the identification rule
Yes, social media is exempt from print ad rules
No, team names are prohibited in all ads
questions.explanation: While team names are allowed in New York advertising, the licensed broker's name must always appear alongside. Social media ads follow the same rules as print advertising.

questions.question_text: Under the NYC FARE Act effective June 2025, who is responsible for paying the broker's commission?
questions.correct_answer: The party who hired the broker
distractors: The buyer in every residential transaction
The seller regardless of who hired the broker
Both parties split the commission equally
questions.explanation: The NYC FARE Act, effective June 2025, establishes that the party who hires the broker pays the commission. This shifts away from the traditional model where sellers typically paid all broker fees.

questions.question_text: A broker creates a print ad that mentions a property in a predominantly minority neighborhood and includes the phrase 'ideal for young professionals.' Which concern does this raise?
questions.correct_answer: Potential fair housing violation through steering
distractors: Failure to include the listing price in the ad
Missing the required MLS cooperation statement
Violation of the blind ad prohibition rule
questions.explanation: All advertising must comply with fair housing laws. Language that targets or excludes groups based on protected characteristics, such as familial status or race, can constitute illegal steering even if unintentional.

questions.question_text: How do New York advertising rules for internet listings compare to print advertising rules?
questions.correct_answer: The same rules apply to both media
distractors: Internet ads require additional DOS approval
Print ads have stricter disclosure requirements
Internet ads are exempt from broker identification
questions.explanation: New York applies the same advertising regulations regardless of medium. Internet and social media advertisements must meet all the same requirements as traditional print advertising, including broker identification and fair housing compliance.

questions.question_text: What is the most critical factor in determining whether a salesperson is an independent contractor or employee in New York?
questions.correct_answer: The degree of control the broker exercises
distractors: Whether the salesperson works full-time or part-time
The existence of a written IC agreement alone
Whether the salesperson has a private office
questions.explanation: Courts apply a substance-over-form test. The actual degree of control the broker exercises over the salesperson's work methods determines status, not just the written agreement. A written IC agreement will not overcome evidence of actual employer control.

questions.question_text: A broker requires all agents to work set hours, attend mandatory daily meetings, and uses a time clock. The written agreement says 'independent contractor.' What is the likely classification?
questions.correct_answer: Employee, because actual control overrides the contract
distractors: Independent contractor per the written agreement
Statutory employee exempt from withholding
Independent contractor if paid by commission only
questions.explanation: Under IRC Section 3508 and NY labor law, substance prevails over form. When a broker directs and controls the manner of work through set hours, mandatory meetings, and time clocks, the salesperson is an employee regardless of what the contract states.

questions.question_text: If a salesperson classified as an independent contractor is reclassified as an employee, which obligation falls on the broker?
distractors: Paying the salesperson a guaranteed salary
Providing a company vehicle for showings
Reimbursing all past marketing expenses
questions.explanation: When a salesperson is reclassified as an employee, the broker becomes liable for unemployment insurance, workers' compensation, and income tax withholding. This can result in significant back-tax liability and penalties.

questions.question_text: Under IRC Section 3508, which condition supports independent contractor status for a real estate salesperson?
questions.correct_answer: Compensation is based on sales output, not hours
distractors: The broker provides all marketing materials
The salesperson works exclusively for one broker
The broker assigns specific territories to each agent
questions.explanation: IRC Section 3508 provides that a licensed real estate agent qualifies as an independent contractor if substantially all compensation is based on sales or output rather than hours worked, and there is a written IC agreement in place.

questions.question_text: What type of written document is required between a broker and an independent contractor salesperson in New York?
distractors: A DOS-approved employment contract
A notarized partnership agreement
An oral agreement witnessed by two licensees
questions.explanation: New York requires a written independent contractor agreement between broker and salesperson. While necessary, this document alone does not guarantee IC status; the actual working relationship must also reflect independent contractor characteristics.

questions.question_text: In New York, what license is required to manage real property for compensation?
distractors: A separate property management certificate
A salesperson's license with a management endorsement
No license if managing fewer than four units
questions.explanation: New York requires anyone who manages property for compensation to hold a real estate license. Property management for pay is considered a real estate activity, and the manager acts as a general agent of the property owner.

questions.question_text: What type of agency relationship does a property manager have with the property owner?
distractors: Special agent
Universal agent
Subagent of the listing broker
questions.explanation: A property manager is a general agent of the property owner, authorized to perform a range of ongoing acts related to the management of the property. This is broader than a special agent, who handles only a single transaction.

questions.question_text: A salesperson is holding an open house alone in a vacant property. Which safety practice is most important?
questions.correct_answer: Have an escape route and tell someone your location
distractors: Keep all exterior doors securely locked during the entire open house
Only allow pre-approved and pre-screened buyers to enter the property
Carry personal defense tools and pepper spray at all times
questions.explanation: Licensee safety at open houses and vacant properties is a recognized concern. Best practices include always informing someone of your whereabouts, having a clear exit route, and avoiding being cornered in isolated areas of a property.

questions.question_text: A property manager stores tenant Social Security numbers on an unencrypted laptop that is stolen. What area of liability does this implicate?
distractors: Fair housing discrimination in tenant screening
Commingling of client escrow funds
Unauthorized practice of real estate law
questions.explanation: Property managers have a duty to protect client and tenant personal information. Storing sensitive data like Social Security numbers without proper cybersecurity measures creates liability for data breaches and violates information protection obligations.

questions.question_text: Which document establishes the relationship between a property owner and property manager?
distractors: A listing agreement with the local MLS
A tenant lease signed by the manager
A DOS registration of agency form
questions.explanation: The property management agreement is a contract that defines the duties, authority, compensation, and responsibilities between the property owner and the manager. It establishes the general agency relationship and protects both parties.
